text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

PATIENT: Castellano, Diane
DOB: [ON FILE]
MRN: [ON FILE]
ACCESSION #: SP-XXXXXXXX
ORDERING PROVIDER: [Surgical Oncology]
SPECIMEN RECEIVED: [DATE]
SPECIMEN REPORTED: [DATE + 2 DAYS]

---

SPECIMEN(S) SUBMITTED:
  A. Wide local excision, left back primary melanoma
  B. Left axillary sentinel lymph node #1
  C. Left axillary sentinel lymph node #2
  D. Left axillary sentinel lymph node #3
  E. Left axillary sentinel lymph node #4

---

CLINICAL HISTORY:
A 55-year-old female with a recently biopsied lesion on the left back consistent with malignant melanoma on prior shave/punch biopsy. Referred for wide local excision and sentinel lymph node biopsy. Prior biopsy report available for correlation.

---

GROSS DESCRIPTION:

Specimen A (Wide local excision, left back):
Received fresh, labeled "wide local excision, left back," is an elliptical excision of skin and subcutaneous tissue measuring 7.5 x 4.2 cm in surface dimension and 2.8 cm in depth. The overlying epidermis demonstrates a centrally located irregular pigmented lesion measuring 1.8 x 1.4 cm. The lesion has irregular borders with areas of tan-brown to dark brown-black discoloration, with a central focus of irregular surface texture. A suture is noted at the 12 o'clock position per operative designation. Inked: peripheral margins black, deep margin blue. The specimen is serially sectioned perpendicular to the long axis. On cut section, the lesion infiltrates to approximately 3.1 mm depth from the granular layer. Subcutaneous tissue appears unremarkable. Representative sections submitted in cassettes A1–A10, including sections of lesion with maximal depth, closest peripheral margin, and deep margin.

Specimen B (Sentinel lymph node #1, left axilla):
Received in formalin, labeled "sentinel lymph node #1, left axilla," is a single tan-pink ovoid lymph node measuring 1.8 x 1.2 x 0.9 cm. The cut surface reveals a pale tan, lobulated parenchyma with no grossly visible metastatic deposits. The node is submitted entirely after bisecting along the long axis, cassettes B1–B3.

Specimen C (Sentinel lymph node #2, left axilla):
Received in formalin, labeled "sentinel lymph node #2, left axilla," is a single tan-pink lymph node measuring 1.1 x 0.8 x 0.6 cm. Entirely submitted, cassette C1.

Specimen D (Sentinel lymph node #3, left axilla):
Received in formalin, labeled "sentinel lymph node #3, left axilla," is a single tan-pink lymph node measuring 0.9 x 0.7 x 0.5 cm. Entirely submitted, cassette D1.

Specimen E (Sentinel lymph node #4, left axilla):
Received in formalin, labeled "sentinel lymph node #4, left axilla," is a single tan-pink lymph node measuring 1.0 x 0.7 x 0.5 cm. Entirely submitted, cassette E1.

---

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:

Specimen A — Wide Local Excision, Left Back:
Sections show malignant melanoma, superficial spreading type, with radial and vertical growth phases. Neoplastic melanocytes are present as a confluent intraepidermal proliferation along the dermal-epidermal junction with irregular upward pagetoid spread extending to the granular and spinous layers. The invasive component consists of irregular nests and fascicles of epithelioid melanocytes with abundant pale to eosinophilic cytoplasm, pleomorphic vesicular nuclei, and prominent eosinophilic nucleoli. Occasional spindle-cell morphology is noted at the advancing deep edge. Mitotic figures are readily identified and number 8 per mm2 at the point of maximal mitotic activity (hotspot counting per CAP protocol). Tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (TILs) are brisk, with a dense band-like lymphocytic infiltrate throughout the base of the tumor. Ulceration of the overlying epidermis is present, with fibrinous exudate and acute inflammatory cells. Microsatellitosis is not identified. Lymphovascular invasion is present: a focus of intravascular neoplastic cells is identified in dermal vessels adjacent to the main tumor mass. Perineural invasion is not identified. Regression: no areas of fibrosis, melanophage aggregates, or telangiectasia consistent with regression are identified. Breslow thickness: 3.1 mm (measured from the top of the granular layer to the deepest invasive melanocyte per AJCC 8th edition methodology). Clark level IV (invasion into reticular dermis). Peripheral margins: the closest peripheral margin is 1.2 cm from the neoplasm (uninvolved). Deep margin: negative; deep resection margin is clear by at least 4 mm.

Specimen B — Sentinel Lymph Node #1, Left Axilla:
Sections show a lymph node with a subcapsular, focally parenchymal deposit of metastatic melanoma. The deposit measures 2.8 mm in greatest dimension. The neoplastic cells are arranged in loose nests and individual cells with the same epithelioid cytomorphology as the primary tumor. No extracapsular extension is identified. The background lymph node parenchyma shows reactive follicular hyperplasia.

Specimen C — Sentinel Lymph Node #2, Left Axilla:
Sections show a lymph node with a small parenchymal deposit of metastatic melanoma measuring approximately 0.9 mm in greatest dimension, present within the lymph node parenchyma. No subcapsular extension identified. No extranodal extension.

Specimen D — Sentinel Lymph Node #3, Left Axilla:
Sections show reactive lymph node. No evidence of metastatic melanoma by hematoxylin and eosin examination. See immunohistochemistry results below.

Specimen E — Sentinel Lymph Node #4, Left Axilla:
Sections show reactive lymph node. No evidence of metastatic melanoma by hematoxylin and eosin examination. See immunohistochemistry results below.

---

ANCILLARY STUDIES — IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:

Primary tumor (Specimen A):
  S-100 protein: POSITIVE — diffuse, strong nuclear and cytoplasmic staining
  Melan-A (MART-1): POSITIVE — diffuse, strong cytoplasmic staining
  HMB-45: POSITIVE — strong staining in superficial and intermediate portions of the vertical growth phase component; focal in deepest zone (expected pattern)
  SOX10: POSITIVE — diffuse, strong nuclear staining
  Ki-67 (MIB-1): Approximately 30% of tumor nuclei positive
  BRAF V600E (clone VE1): POSITIVE — diffuse, strong cytoplasmic staining in invasive neoplastic cells; pattern consistent with BRAF V600E substitution

Sentinel lymph nodes (Specimens B–E), IHC panel (S-100, Melan-A):
  Specimen B (SLN #1): POSITIVE for metastatic melanoma — deposit confirmed by S-100 and Melan-A
  Specimen C (SLN #2): POSITIVE for metastatic melanoma — small parenchymal deposit confirmed by S-100 and Melan-A
  Specimen D (SLN #3): NEGATIVE — no immunohistochemically detectable metastatic melanoma
  Specimen E (SLN #4): NEGATIVE — no immunohistochemically detectable metastatic melanoma

Note: BRAF mutation confirmation by molecular testing (PCR or NGS) is recommended and is pending/being processed separately through the molecular diagnostics laboratory.

---

DIAGNOSTIC SUMMARY:

A. WIDE LOCAL EXCISION, LEFT BACK:
   — Malignant melanoma, superficial spreading type
   — Breslow thickness: 3.1 mm
   — Clark level: IV
   — Ulceration: PRESENT
   — Mitotic rate: 8 mitoses/mm2
   — Lymphovascular invasion: PRESENT
   — Perineural invasion: ABSENT
   — Microsatellitosis: ABSENT
   — Tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes: BRISK
   — Regression: ABSENT
   — Peripheral margins: NEGATIVE (closest margin 1.2 cm)
   — Deep margin: NEGATIVE
   — BRAF V600E by IHC (VE1 clone): POSITIVE

B. LEFT AXILLARY SENTINEL LYMPH NODE #1:
   — Metastatic melanoma present
   — Deposit size: 2.8 mm (subcapsular/parenchymal)
   — Extranodal extension: ABSENT

C. LEFT AXILLARY SENTINEL LYMPH NODE #2:
   — Metastatic melanoma present
   — Deposit size: 0.9 mm (parenchymal)
   — Extranodal extension: ABSENT

D. LEFT AXILLARY SENTINEL LYMPH NODE #3:
   — No metastatic melanoma identified (H&E and IHC)

E. LEFT AXILLARY SENTINEL LYMPH NODE #4:
   — No metastatic melanoma identified (H&E and IHC)

Sentinel lymph node summary: 2 of 4 left axillary sentinel lymph nodes positive for metastatic melanoma.

---

PATHOLOGIC STAGING (AJCC 8th Edition, pathologic staging contribution from this specimen):
   pT3b (Breslow thickness 2.0–4.0 mm, with ulceration)
   pN2a (2 sentinel lymph nodes with micrometastases)

   Note: Clinical/radiographic M staging (M1c) is determined in conjunction with clinical and imaging findings and is not assessed on this specimen.

---

ATTENDING PATHOLOGIST: [Attending Pathologist, MD]
Resident/Fellow: [Fellow, MD]
Electronically signed: [DATE/TIME]

Case reviewed at multidisciplinary conference: PENDING

[END OF REPORT]
